Really poor days racing for a Friday. Brief reviews as they're class 6 outsiders and more likely to come last than first.

3.10 Carlisle MIDNIGHT LIR 10/1 0.75pt ew

3.20 Brighton GALLANT LION 7/1 0.75pt ew

0.5pt Each Way Double

MIDNIGHT LIR is exceptionally well handicapped if he can come back to anywhere near his best, but has clearly had his fair share of issues over the last couple of years. Once quite hardy, he's struggled to get to the racetrack for the last two seasons and his form has, understandably been poor. However, he's managed to put two runs together, provided he runs, for the first time in a year and hopefully his issues are behind him. Racing off a mark of 61, he's 22lb lower than his last winning mark and 25lb lower than his peak. Michael Dods, whose horse are in great form, has applied the first time blinkers for this lad and has a great record with the headgear first time. A stiff 5f, which Carlisle is, is perfect and his draw in stall 2 ought to be ideal on quick ground.

GALLANT LION has been awful for a while, but he did land a huge gamble on the 30th May last year and has now dropped to a mark that is lower than for that win. A course winner too, Ian Williams has booked Jason Watson (who has won on him before) for the ride. Theres a lot to take on trust, and this horse is forecast to open up as the 20/1 rank outsider in this race, but his run over a mile last time out was clearly used for fitness as he'd not be fast enough for a mile in the back of a horse box. Ian's horses are in good form and this is his only runner at the track today. Jason Watson is riding superbly right now.
